expectations.text: Frames the empty state as an important design opportunity, not just a fallback
expectations.evidence: 'Don't Overlook Empty States — a blank white area is a missed design opportunity, often the first thing new users see'; also: 'Treat Every Empty State as a Feature: Empty states aren't an afterthought — they're a product design moment'

expectations.text: Mentions at least two of the three components: acknowledging emptiness, explaining what will appear, providing a call-to-action
expectations.evidence: All three listed explicitly: '1. Acknowledge that nothing is here yet; 2. Explain what will appear here; 3. Offer a clear call-to-action to create the first item'

expectations.text: Provides copy guidance with examples or principles (avoid terse messages like 'No items found')
expectations.evidence: Direct contrast example: '❌ No items found. ✅ You haven't added anything yet. Create your first one to get started.' — exactly the principle + negative/positive example format called for

eval_feedback.overall: Solid. All three pass cleanly. The ❌/✅ copy contrast is a strong, memorable teaching moment.
